How does Ethereum work, anyway?

READ STORY
Written On
September 13, 2017
in
Blockchain写于2017年9月13日，分类于区块链

Odds are you’ve heard about the Ethereum blockchain, whether or not you know what it is. It’s been in the news a lot lately, including the cover of some major magazines, but reading those articles can be like gibberish if you don’t have a foundation for what exactly Ethereum is. So what is it? In essence, a public database that keeps a permanent record of digital transactions. Importantly, this database doesn’t require any central authority to maintain and secure it. Instead it operates as a “trustless” transactional system — a framework in which individuals can make peer-to-peer transactions without needing to trust a third party OR one another.很可能你听说过以太坊区块链，不管你是否清楚它究竟是什么。最近它频频出现在新闻中，甚至登上了一些主流杂志的封面，但如果你不了解以太坊的本质，读这些文章可能会觉得不知所云。那么它到底是什么？本质上，它是一个永久记录数字交易的公共数据库。重要的是，这个数据库无需任何中央机构来维护和保障安全。相反，它是一个“无需信任”的交易系统——在这个框架下，个人可以进行点对点交易，无需信任第三方，也无需彼此信任。

Still confused? That’s where this post comes in. My aim is to explain how Ethereum functions at a technical level, without complex math or scary-looking formulas. Even if you’re not a programmer, I hope you’ll walk away with at least better grasp of the tech. If some parts are too technical and difficult to grok, that’s totally fine! There’s really no need to understand every little detail. I recommend just focusing on understanding things at a broad level.还在困惑吗？这篇文章就是为了解决这个问题而来的。我的目标是从技术层面解释以太坊的运作原理，且不涉及复杂的数学公式或看起来晦涩的公式。即便你不是程序员，我也希望你读完后能至少对这项技术有更清晰的认识。如果有些部分过于专业、难以理解，完全没关系！其实没必要搞懂每一个细节。我建议你只需从宏观层面去理解即可。

Many of the topics covered in this post are a breakdown of the concepts discussed in the yellow paper. I’ve added my own explanations and diagrams to make understanding Ethereum easier. Those brave enough to take on the technical challenge can also read the Ethereum yellow paper.这篇文章涉及的诸多主题都是对黄皮书中讨论的概念的拆解。我加入了自己的解释和图表，以帮助大家更轻松地理解以太坊。有勇气迎接技术挑战的读者也可以阅读《以太坊黄皮书》。

Let’s get started! 让我们开始吧！

PS: If you are a developer interested in learning how to build Dapps on Ethereum, join my NEW cohort-based course.附：如果你是一名有兴趣学习如何在以太坊上构建去中心化应用（Dapps）的开发者，欢迎加入我的全新的基于学习小组的课程。

Blockchain definition 区块链定义

A blockchain is a “cryptographically secure transactional singleton machine with shared-state.” [1] That’s a mouthful, isn’t it? Let’s break it down.区块链是一种“具有共享状态的、密码学安全的事务性单例机器”[1]。这听起来很拗口，对吧？我们来拆解一下它的含义。

  • “Cryptographically secure” means that the creation of digital currency is secured by complex mathematical algorithms that are obscenely hard to break. Think of a firewall of sorts. They make it nearly impossible to cheat the system (e.g. create fake transactions, erase transactions, etc.)“密码学安全”指数字货币的创建由极其难以破解的复杂数学算法提供保障。可以将其视为一种防火墙。这些算法使得系统几乎不可能被作弊（例如伪造交易、删除交易等）。
  • “Transactional singleton machine” means that there’s a single canonical instance of the machine responsible for all the transactions being created in the system. In other words, there’s a single global truth that everyone believes in.“事务单例机器”指的是系统中负责创建所有事务的机器只有一个规范实例。换句话说，存在一个所有人都认可的单一全局事实。
  • “With shared-state” means that the state stored on this machine is shared and open to everyone.“共享状态”</b>指的是存储在这台机器上的状态对所有人都是共享且开放的。

Ethereum implements this blockchain paradigm.以太坊实现了这一区块链范式。

The Ethereum blockchain paradigm explained以太坊区块链范式解析

The Ethereum blockchain is essentially a transaction-based state machine. In computer science, a state machine refers to something that will read a series of inputs and, based on those inputs, will transition to a new state.以太坊区块链本质上是一个基于交易的状态机。在计算机科学中，状态机指的是一种会读取一系列输入，并根据这些输入转换到新状态的装置。

With Ethereum’s state machine, we begin with a “genesis state.” This is analogous to a blank slate, before any transactions have happened on the network. When transactions are executed, this genesis state transitions into some final state. At any point in time, this final state represents the current state of Ethereum.借助以太坊的状态机，我们从“创世状态”开始。这就好比网络上尚未发生任何交易时的一张白纸。当交易执行时，这个创世状态会过渡到某个最终状态。在任意时间点，该最终状态都代表着以太坊的当前状态。

The state of Ethereum has millions of transactions. These transactions are grouped into “blocks.” A block contains a series of transactions, and each block is chained together with its previous block.以太坊网络中存在数百万笔交易。这些交易被分组为“区块”。一个区块包含一系列交易，且每个区块都与其前一个区块相连形成链状结构。

To cause a transition from one state to the next, a transaction must be valid. For a transaction to be considered valid, it must go through a validation process known as mining. Mining is when a group of nodes (i.e. computers) expend their compute resources to create a block of valid transactions.要实现从一个状态到下一个状态的转换，交易必须有效。一笔交易要被视为有效，必须经过一个被称为挖矿的验证流程。挖矿是指一组节点（即计算机）消耗其计算资源来生成一个包含有效交易的区块。

Any node on the network that declares itself as a miner can attempt to create and validate a block. Lots of miners from around the world try to create and validate blocks at the same time. Each miner provides a mathematical “proof” when submitting a block to the blockchain, and this proof acts as a guarantee: if the proof exists, the block must be valid.网络上任何宣称自己为矿工的节点都可以尝试创建并验证区块。来自全球的众多矿工同时尝试创建和验证区块。每位矿工在向区块链提交区块时都会提供一份数学“证明”，该证明可作为保证：若证明存在，则该区块必然有效。

For a block to be added to the main blockchain, the miner must prove it faster than any other competitor miner. The process of validating each block by having a miner provide a mathematical proof is known as a “proof of work.”要将一个区块添加到主区块链中，矿工必须比其他所有竞争矿工更快地完成证明。由矿工提供数学证明来验证每个区块的过程被称为“工作量证明”。

A miner who validates a new block is rewarded with a certain amount of value for doing this work. What is that value? The Ethereum blockchain uses an intrinsic digital token called “Ether.” Every time a miner proves a block, new Ether tokens are generated and awarded.验证新区块的矿工因完成这项工作会获得一定数量的价值奖励。这一价值是什么？以太坊区块链有一种名为“以太币”的原生数字代币。每当矿工验证出一个区块，新的以太币就会被生成并发放给该矿工。

You might wonder: what guarantees that everyone sticks to one chain of blocks? How can we be sure that there doesn’t exist a subset of miners who will decide to create their own chain of blocks?你可能会好奇：是什么保证所有人都只遵循一条区块链？我们又如何确定不会有一部分矿工决定创建自己的区块链呢？

Earlier, we defined a blockchain as a transactional singleton machine with shared-state. Using this definition, we can understand the correct current state is a single global truth, which everyone must accept. Having multiple states (or chains) would ruin the whole system, because it would be impossible to agree on which state was the correct one. If the chains were to diverge, you might own 10 coins on one chain, 20 on another, and 40 on another. In this scenario, there would be no way to determine which chain was the most “valid.”此前，我们将区块链定义为一台具有共享状态的事务单例机器。根据这一定义，我们可以明白，正确的当前状态是唯一的全局事实，所有人都必须予以认可。存在多个状态（或多条链）会破坏整个系统，因为人们无法就哪个状态是正确的达成一致。如果链发生分叉，你可能在一条链上拥有10枚代币，在另一条链上拥有20枚，在第三条链上拥有40枚。在这种情况下，无法判定哪条链最具“有效性”。

Whenever multiple paths are generated, a “fork” occurs. We typically want to avoid forks, because they disrupt the system and force people to choose which chain they “believe” in.每当生成多条路径时，就会出现一次“分叉”。我们通常希望避免分叉，因为分叉会扰乱系统，并迫使人们选择他们所“信任”的链。

To determine which path is most valid and prevent multiple chains, Ethereum uses a mechanism called the “GHOST protocol.”为了确定哪条路径最有效并防止出现多条区块链，以太坊采用了一种名为“GHOST协议”的机制。

“GHOST” = “Greedy Heaviest Observed Subtree”“GHOST” = “贪婪最重观测子树”

In simple terms, the GHOST protocol says we must pick the path that has had the most computation done upon it. One way to determine that path is to use the block number of the most recent block (the “leaf block”), which represents the total number of blocks in the current path (not counting the genesis block). The higher the block number, the longer the path and the greater the mining effort that must have gone into arriving at the leaf. Using this reasoning allows us to agree on the canonical version of the current state.简单来说，GHOST 协议规定我们必须选择计算量最大的那条链。确定这条链的一种方法是使用最新区块（即“叶子区块”）的区块号，它代表了当前链上的区块总数（不包含创世区块）。区块号越高，意味着链越长，生成该叶子区块所投入的挖矿工作量也就越大。基于这一原理，我们就能就当前状态的规范版本达成一致。

Now that you’ve gotten the 10,000-foot overview of what a blockchain is, let’s dive deeper into the main components that the Ethereum system is comprised of:既然你已经对区块链是什么有了一个宏观的整体认识，接下来我们深入探讨以太坊系统所包含的主要组成部分：

  • accounts 账户
  • state 状态
  • gas and fees 燃气与费用
  • transactions 交易
  • blocks 区块
  • transaction execution 交易执行
  • mining 挖矿
  • proof of work 工作量证明

One note before getting started: whenever I say “hash” of X, I am referring to the KECCAK-256 hash, which Ethereum uses.开始前先说明一点：每当我提到 X 的“哈希”时，指的都是 KECCAK-256 哈希，这是以太坊所使用的哈希算法。

Accounts 账户

The global “shared-state” of Ethereum is comprised of many small objects (“accounts”) that are able to interact with one another through a message-passing framework. Each account has a state associated with it and a 20-byte address. An address in Ethereum is a 160-bit identifier that is used to identify any account.以太坊的全局“共享状态”由许多能够通过消息传递框架相互交互的小型对象（“账户”）组成。每个账户都有一个与之关联的状态</b>和一个20字节的地址。以太坊中的地址是一个160位的标识符，用于标识任何账户。

There are two types of accounts: 有两种类型的帐户：

  • Externally owned accounts, which are controlled by private keys and have no code associated with them.外部拥有的账户由私钥控制，且没有与之关联的代码。
  • Contract accounts, which are controlled by their contract code and have code associated with them.合同账户由其合同代码控制，且关联有相应代码。

Externally owned accounts vs. contract accounts外部拥有账户与合约账户

It’s important to understand a fundamental difference between externally owned accounts and contract accounts. An externally owned account can send messages to other externally owned accounts OR to other contract accounts by creating and signing a transaction using its private key. A message between two externally owned accounts is simply a value transfer. But a message from an externally owned account to a contract account activates the contract account’s code, allowing it to perform various actions (e.g. transfer tokens, write to internal storage, mint new tokens, perform some calculation, create new contracts, etc.).理解外部拥有账户与合约账户之间的根本差异至关重要。外部拥有账户可通过使用私钥创建并签署交易，向其他外部拥有账户或其他合约账户发送消息。两个外部拥有账户之间的消息仅是价值转移。而外部拥有账户向合约账户发送的消息会激活合约账户的代码，使其能够执行各类操作（例如转移通证、写入内部存储、铸造新通证、执行特定计算、创建新合约等）。

Unlike externally owned accounts, contract accounts can’t initiate new transactions on their own. Instead, contract accounts can only fire transactions in response to other transactions they have received (from an externally owned account or from another contract account). We’ll learn more about contract-to-contract calls in the “Transactions and Messages” section.与外部拥有的账户不同，合约账户无法自行发起新交易。 相反，合约账户只能在收到其他交易（来自外部拥有的账户或另一个合约账户）后触发交易。我们将在交易与消息一节中详细了解合约间的调用。

Therefore, any action that occurs on the Ethereum blockchain is always set in motion by transactions fired from externally controlled accounts.因此，以太坊区块链上发生的任何操作，始终都是由外部控制账户发起的交易来触发的。

Account state 账户状态

The account state consists of four components, which are present regardless of the type of account:账户状态包含四个组成部分，无论账户类型如何，这四个部分均存在：

  • nonce: If the account is an externally owned account, this number represents the number of transactions sent from the account’s address. If the account is a contract account, the nonce is the number of contracts created by the account.随机数：若账户为外部拥有账户，此数字表示从该账户地址发送的交易数量。若账户为合约账户，随机数则为该账户创建的合约数量。
  • balance: The number of Wei owned by this address. There are 1e+18 Wei per Ether.余额：该地址拥有的 Wei 数量。每 1 个以太币等于 1e+18 个 Wei。
  • storageRoot: A hash of the root node of a Merkle Patricia tree (we’ll explain Merkle trees later on). This tree encodes the hash of the storage contents of this account, and is empty by default.存储根：默克尔-帕特里夏树根节点的哈希值（我们稍后会解释默克尔树）。该树对本账户存储内容的哈希值进行编码，默认情况下为空。
  • codeHash: The hash of the EVM (Ethereum Virtual Machine — more on this later) code of this account. For contract accounts, this is the code that gets hashed and stored as the codeHash. For externally owned accounts, the codeHash field is the hash of the empty string.代码哈希：此账户的 EVM（以太坊虚拟机——后续会详细说明）代码的哈希值。对于合约账户，这是被哈希处理并存储为 代码哈希 的代码。对于外部拥有账户，代码哈希 字段是空字符串的哈希值。

World state 世界状态

Okay, so we know that Ethereum’s global state consists of a mapping between account addresses and the account states. This mapping is stored in a data structure known as a Merkle Patricia tree.好的，我们知道以太坊的全局状态由账户地址与账户状态之间的映射关系构成。这种映射关系存储在一种名为默克尔帕特里夏树的数据结构中。

A Merkle tree (or also referred as “Merkle trie”) is a type of binary tree composed of a set of nodes with:默克尔树（也被称为“默克尔树”）是一种由一组节点组成的二叉树，具有以下特征：

  • a large number of leaf nodes at the bottom of the tree that contain the underlying data树底部有大量包含底层数据的叶节点
  • a set of intermediate nodes, where each node is the hash of its two child nodes一组中间节点，其中每个节点都是其两个子节点的哈希值
  • a single root node, also formed from the hash of its two child node, representing the top of the tree单个根节点，同样由其两个子节点的哈希值生成，代表该树的顶部

The data at the bottom of the tree is generated by splitting the data that we want to store into chunks, then splitting the chunks into buckets, and then taking the hash of each bucket and repeating the same process until the total number of hashes remaining becomes only one: the root hash.树底部的数据是通过将我们要存储的数据拆分为数据块，再将数据块拆分为存储桶，然后对每个存储桶取哈希值并重复这一过程，直到剩余的哈希值总数只剩一个——根哈希值，从而生成的。

This tree is required to have a key for every value stored inside it. Beginning from the root node of the tree, the key should tell you which child node to follow to get to the corresponding value, which is stored in the leaf nodes. In Ethereum’s case, the key/value mapping for the state tree is between addresses and their associated accounts, including the balance, nonce, codeHash, and storageRoot for each account (where the storageRoot is itself a tree).这棵树要求为其中存储的每个值都配备一个键。从树的根节点开始，该键应当能指引你找到对应的子节点，进而获取存储在叶节点中的对应值。在以太坊中，状态树的键值映射建立在地址与其关联账户之间，每个账户包含余额、随机数（nonce）、代码哈希（codeHash）以及存储根（storageRoot，而存储根本身也是一棵树）。

This same trie structure is used also to store transactions and receipts. More specifically, every block has a “header” which stores the hash of the root node of three different Merkle trie structures, including:同样的前缀树结构也用于存储交易和收据。更具体地说，每个区块都有一个“区块头”，用于存储三种不同的默克尔前缀树结构的根节点哈希，具体包括：

  1. State trie 状态树
  2. Transactions trie 交易树
  3. Receipts trie 收据树

The ability to store all this information efficiently in Merkle tries is incredibly useful in Ethereum for what we call “light clients” or “light nodes.” Remember that a blockchain is maintained by a bunch of nodes. Broadly speaking, there are two types of nodes: full nodes and light nodes.在以太坊中，能够在默克尔树中高效存储所有这些信息，对我们所说的“轻客户端”或“轻节点”而言极具实用价值。要知道，区块链由众多节点维护。广义来说，节点分为两类：全节点和轻节点。

A full archive node synchronizes the blockchain by downloading the full chain, from the genesis block to the current head block, executing all of the transactions contained within. Typically, miners store the full archive node, because they are required to do so for the mining process. It is also possible to download a full node without executing every transaction. Regardless, any full node contains the entire chain.全归档节点通过下载从创世区块到当前头部区块的完整区块链，并执行其中包含的所有交易来实现区块链同步。通常，矿工会存储全归档节点，因为挖矿流程要求他们必须这么做。也可以下载全节点而不执行每一笔交易。无论如何，任何全节点都包含完整的区块链。

But unless a node needs to execute every transaction or easily query historical data, there’s really no need to store the entire chain. This is where the concept of a light node comes in. Instead of downloading and storing the full chain and executing all of the transactions, light nodes download only the chain of headers, from the genesis block to the current head, without executing any transactions or retrieving any associated state. Because light nodes have access to block headers, which contain hashes of three tries, they can still easily generate and receive verifiable answers about transactions, events, balances, etc.但除非某个节点需要执行每笔交易或轻松查询历史数据，否则实际上无需存储整条链。这就是轻节点概念的用武之地。轻节点无需下载并存储完整链、执行所有交易，而是仅下载从创世区块到当前头部的头部链，不执行任何交易，也不获取任何相关状态。由于轻节点可访问包含三个默克尔树哈希的区块头，它们仍能轻松生成并获取关于交易、事件、余额等信息的可验证答案。

The reason this works is because hashes in the Merkle tree propagate upward — if a malicious user attempts to swap a fake transaction into the bottom of a Merkle tree, this change will cause a change in the hash of the node above, which will change the hash of the node above that, and so on, until it eventually changes the root of the tree.之所以这种方法可行，是因为默克尔树中的哈希值会向上传递——如果恶意用户试图将伪造的交易替换到默克尔树的底部，这一改动会导致上方节点的哈希值发生变化，进而引发上一层节点的哈希值改变，以此类推，最终会改变整棵树的根哈希值。

Any node that wants to verify a piece of data can use something called a “Merkle proof” to do so. A Merkle proof consists of:任何想要验证某段数据的节点都可以使用一种名为“默克尔证明”的方法来完成。默克尔证明包含以下内容：

  1. A chunk of data to be verified and its hash待验证的数据块及其哈希值
  2. The root hash of the tree 树的根哈希
  3. The “branch” (all of the partner hashes going up along the path from the chunk to the root)“分支”（即从数据块到根节点路径上所有的同伴哈希值）

Anyone reading the proof can verify that the hashing for that branch is consistent all the way up the tree, and therefore that the given chunk is actually at that position in the tree.任何阅读该证明的人都可以验证，该分支的哈希在整棵树中始终保持一致，因此给定的数据块实际上位于树中的该位置。

In summary, the benefit of using a Merkle Patricia tree is that the root node of this structure is cryptographically dependent on the data stored in the tree, and so the hash of the root node can be used as a secure identity for this data. Since the block header includes the root hash of the state, transactions, and receipts trees, any node can validate a small part of state of Ethereum without needing to store the entire state, which can be potentially unbounded in size.总而言之，使用帕特里夏默克尔树的优势在于，该结构的根节点与存储在树中的数据存在密码学上的依赖关系，因此根节点的哈希值可以作为该数据的安全标识。由于区块头包含了状态树、交易树和收据树的根哈希，任何节点都无需存储整个状态（其大小可能无限），即可验证以太坊状态的一小部分。

Gas and payment Gas 与支付

One very important concept in Ethereum is the concept of fees. Every computation that occurs as a result of a transaction on the Ethereum network incurs a fee — there’s no free lunch! This fee is paid in a denomination called “gas.”以太坊中一个非常重要的概念是费用概念。以太坊网络上交易引发的每一次计算都会产生一笔费用——天下没有免费的午餐！ 这笔费用以名为“Gas”的计价单位支付。

Gas is the unit used to measure the fees required for a particular computation. Gas price is the amount of Ether you are willing to spend on every unit of gas, and is measured in “gwei.” “Wei” is the smallest unit of Ether, where 1⁰¹⁸ Wei represents 1 Ether. One gwei is 1,000,000,000 Wei.Gas是用于衡量特定计算所需费用的单位。Gas价格是你愿意为每单位<b>Gas</b>支付的以太币金额，以“gwei”为单位。“Wei”是以太币的最小单位，10¹⁸个Wei等于1个以太币。1个gwei等于10亿个Wei。

With every transaction, a sender sets a gas limit and gas price. The product of gas price and gas limit represents the maximum amount of Wei that the sender is willing to pay for executing a transaction.每笔交易中，发送方都会设定一个燃气上限和燃气价格。燃气价格与燃气上限的乘积代表发送方愿意为执行一笔交易支付的最大以太币单位（Wei）数量。

For example, let’s say the sender sets the gas limit to 50,000 and a gas price to 20 gwei. This implies that the sender is willing to spend at most 50,000 x 20 gwei = 1,000,000,000,000,000 Wei = 0.001 Ether to execute that transaction.例如，假设发送方将燃气限制设置为 50,000，燃气价格设置为 20 吉威。这意味着发送方愿意为执行该笔交易最多花费 50,000 × 20 吉威 = 1,000,000,000,000,000 韦 = 0.001 以太币。

Remember that the gas limit represents the maximum gas the sender is willing to spend money on. If they have enough Ether in their account balance to cover this maximum, they’re good to go. The sender is refunded for any unused gas at the end of the transaction, exchanged at the original rate.请记住，燃气限额代表发送方愿意花钱支付的最大燃气量。如果其账户余额中有足够的以太币来支付这一最大额度，交易即可顺利进行。交易结束后，未使用的燃气会按原汇率退还给发送方。

In the case that the sender does not provide the necessary gas to execute the transaction, the transaction runs “out of gas” and is considered invalid. In this case, the transaction processing aborts and any state changes that occurred are reversed, such that we end up back at the state of Ethereum prior to the transaction. Additionally, a record of the transaction failing gets recorded, showing what transaction was attempted and where it failed. And since the machine already expended effort to run the calculations before running out of gas, logically, none of the gas is refunded to the sender.如果发送方未提供执行交易所需的燃气，交易将出现“燃气耗尽”情况并被视为无效。此时，交易处理会中止，所有已发生的状态更改都将被撤销，最终恢复到交易执行前的以太坊状态。此外，系统会记录交易失败的相关信息，显示尝试执行的交易内容及其失败位置。由于虚拟机在燃气耗尽前已耗费算力完成了部分计算，因此从逻辑上讲，发送方不会获得任何燃气退款。

Where exactly does this gas money go? All the money spent on gas by the sender is sent to the “beneficiary” address, which is typically the miner’s address. Since miners are expending the effort to run computations and validate transactions, miners receive the gas fee as a reward.这笔燃气费具体花在了哪里？发送方在燃气上花费的所有资金都会转入“受益人”地址，该地址通常是矿工的地址。由于矿工需要付出精力运行计算程序并验证交易，因此他们会将燃气费作为奖励收入囊中。

Typically, the higher the gas price the sender is willing to pay, the greater the value the miner derives from the transaction. Thus, the more likely miners will be to select it. In this way, miners are free to choose which transactions they want to validate or ignore. In order to guide senders on what gas price to set, miners have the option of advertising the minimum gas price for which they will execute transactions.通常情况下，发送方愿意支付的燃气价格越高，矿工从该交易中获得的价值就越大。因此，矿工选择该交易的可能性也就越高。通过这种方式，矿工可以自由选择他们想要验证或忽略的交易。为了向发送方指导应设置的燃气价格，矿工可以选择公布他们执行交易所要求的最低燃气价格。

There are fees for storage, too 存储也需要支付费用

Not only is gas used to pay for computation steps, it is also used to pay for storage usage. The total fee for storage is proportional to the smallest multiple of 32 bytes used.Gas不仅用于支付计算步骤的费用，还用于支付存储使用的费用。 存储的总费用与所使用的32字节最小倍数成正比。

Fees for storage have some nuanced aspects. For example, since increased storage increases the size of the Ethereum state database on all nodes, there’s an incentive to keep the amount of data stored small. For this reason, if a transaction has a step that clears an entry in the storage, the fee for executing that operation of is waived, AND a refund is given for freeing up storage space.存储费用存在一些复杂的方面。例如，由于存储容量增加会扩大所有节点上的以太坊状态数据库规模，因此存在将存储数据量控制在较小范围内的激励。基于这一原因，如果某笔交易包含清除存储中某一条目的步骤，执行该操作的费用将被免除，同时还会为释放存储空间提供退款。

What’s the purpose of fees? 费用的作用是什么？

One important aspect of the way the Ethereum works is that every single operation executed by the network is simultaneously effected by every full node. However, computational steps on the Ethereum Virtual Machine are very expensive. Therefore, Ethereum smart contracts are best used for simple tasks, like running simple business logic or verifying signatures and other cryptographic objects, rather than more complex uses, like file storage, email, or machine learning, which can put a strain on the network. Imposing fees prevents users from overtaxing the network.以太坊运行方式的一个重要特点是，网络执行的每一个操作都会同时被所有全节点生效。然而，以太坊虚拟机上的计算步骤成本极高。因此，以太坊智能合约最适合用于简单任务，比如运行简单的业务逻辑、验证签名及其他加密对象，而非文件存储、电子邮件或机器学习等更复杂的用途——这类复杂用途会给网络带来压力。收取费用可以防止用户过度占用网络资源。

Ethereum is a Turing complete language. (In short, a Turing machine is a machine that can simulate any computer algorithm (for those not familiar with Turing machines, check out this and this). This allows for loops and makes Ethereum susceptible to the halting problem, a problem in which you cannot determine whether or not a program will run infinitely. If there were no fees, a malicious actor could easily try to disrupt the network by executing an infinite loop within a transaction, without any repercussions. Thus, fees protect the network from deliberate attacks.以太坊是一种图灵完备的语言。（简而言之，图灵机是一种可以模拟任何计算机算法的机器（不熟悉图灵机的读者可查看这个和这个链接）。这一特性支持循环语句的编写，也使得以太坊面临停机问题——即无法判断一个程序是否会无限运行。如果没有交易手续费，恶意攻击者可以轻易在交易中执行无限循环来破坏网络，且不会受到任何惩罚。因此，手续费能保护网络免受蓄意攻击。

You might be thinking, “why do we also have to pay for storage?” Well, just like computation, storage on the Ethereum network is a cost that the entire network has to take the burden of.你可能会想：“为什么我们还得为存储付费呢？”其实，和计算一样，以太坊网络上的存储也是整个网络都需要承担的成本。

Transaction and messages 交易与消息

We noted earlier that Ethereum is a transaction-based state machine. In other words, transactions occurring between different accounts are what move the global state of Ethereum from one state to the next.我们之前提到过，以太坊是一台基于交易的状态机。换句话说，不同账户之间发生的交易，是将以太坊的全局状态从一个状态转移到下一个状态的关键。

In the most basic sense, a transaction is a cryptographically signed piece of instruction that is generated by an externally owned account, serialized, and then submitted to the blockchain.从最基本的意义上来说，交易是由外部拥有账户生成的、经过密码签名的指令片段，它会被序列化后提交至区块链。

There are two types of transactions: message calls and contract creations (i.e. transactions that create new Ethereum contracts). 交易分为两种类型：消息调用和合约创建（即用于创建新以太坊合约的交易）。

All transactions contain the following components, regardless of their type:无论交易类型如何，所有交易都包含以下组成部分：

  • nonce: a count of the number of transactions sent by the sender.随机数：发送方发送的交易数量的计数。
  • gasPrice: the number of Wei that the sender is willing to pay per unit of gas required to execute the transaction.gasPrice：发送方愿意为执行交易所需的每单位燃气支付的 Wei 数量。
  • gasLimit: the maximum amount of gas that the sender is willing to pay for executing this transaction. This amount is set and paid upfront, before any computation is done.燃气限额：发送方愿意为执行该交易支付的最大燃气量。该金额在进行任何计算之前就已预先设定并支付。
  • to: the address of the recipient. In a contract-creating transaction, the contract account address does not yet exist, and so an empty value is used.至：接收方的地址。在创建合约的交易中，合约账户地址尚不存在，因此使用空值。
  • value: the amount of Wei to be transferred from the sender to the recipient. In a contract-creating transaction, this value serves as the starting balance within the newly created contract account.值：要从发送方转移到接收方的 Wei 数量。在创建合约的交易中，该值作为新创建的合约账户内的初始余额。
  • v, r, s: used to generate the signature that identifies the sender of the transaction.v、r、s：用于生成标识交易发送方的签名。
  • init (only exists for contract-creating transactions): An EVM code fragment that is used to initialize the new contract account. init is run only once, and then is discarded. When init is first run, it returns the body of the account code, which is the piece of code that is permanently associated with the contract account.初始化（仅在创建合约的交易中存在）：用于初始化新合约账户的 EVM 代码片段。初始化仅运行一次，随后会被丢弃。当初始化首次运行时，它会返回账户代码的主体，这是与合约账户永久关联的那段代码。
  • data (optional field that only exists for message calls): the input data (i.e. parameters) of the message call. For example, if a smart contract serves as a domain registration service, a call to that contract might expect input fields such as the domain and IP address.数据（可选字段，仅存在于消息调用中）：消息调用的输入数据（即参数）。例如，若某智能合约充当域名注册服务，调用该合约时可能需要域名和IP地址等输入字段。

We learned in the “Accounts” section that transactions — both message calls and contract-creating transactions — are always initiated by externally owned accounts and submitted to the blockchain. Another way to think about it is that transactions are what bridge the external world to the internal state of Ethereum.我们在“账户”部分了解到，交易——包括消息调用和创建合约的交易——始终由外部拥有账户发起并提交至区块链。换个角度理解，交易是连接外部世界与以太坊内部状态的桥梁。

But this doesn’t mean that contracts can’t talk to other contracts. Contracts that exist within the global scope of Ethereum’s state can talk to other contracts within that same scope. The way they do this is via “messages” or “internal transactions” to other contracts. We can think of messages or internal transactions as being similar to transactions, with the major difference that they are NOT generated by externally owned accounts. Instead, they are generated by contracts. They are virtual objects that, unlike transactions, are not serialized and only exist in the Ethereum execution environment.但这并不意味着合约无法与其他合约交互。存在于以太坊状态全局范围内的合约，可以与同一范围内的其他合约进行交互。它们实现这一交互的方式，是向其他合约发送“消息”或“内部交易”。我们可以将消息或内部交易视为与交易类似的形式，主要区别在于它们并非由外部拥有账户生成。相反，它们由合约生成。与交易不同，这些消息或内部交易是未被序列化的虚拟对象，仅存在于以太坊执行环境中。

When one contract sends an internal transaction to another contract, the associated code that exists on the recipient contract account is executed.当一个合约向另一个合约发送内部交易时，接收方合约账户上存在的相关代码将被执行。

One important thing to note is that internal transactions or messages don’t contain a gasLimit. This is because the gas limit is determined by the external creator of the original transaction (i.e. some externally owned account). The gas limit that the externally owned account sets must be high enough to carry out the transaction, including any sub-executions that occur as a result of that transaction, such as contract-to-contract messages. If, in the chain of transactions and messages, a particular message execution runs out of gas, then that message’s execution will revert, along with any previous messages triggered by the execution. The only exception to this is if you use the raw CALL opcode within the contract (which is not recommended unless you absolutely know what you are doing). In this case, a CALL to another contract can fail, but if the parent contract doesn't check and handle that error, then execution proceeds as normal and the TX result could show success.需要注意的一点重要事项是，内部交易或消息不包含gasLimit（燃气上限）。这是因为燃气上限由原始交易的外部创建者（即某一外部拥有账户）确定。外部拥有账户设置的燃气上限必须足够高，以完成该交易的执行，包括该交易所引发的所有子执行操作，例如合约与合约之间的消息传递。如果在交易和消息的执行链条中，某一特定消息的执行耗尽了燃气，那么该消息的执行将回滚，且由该执行触发的所有先前消息也会一并回滚。唯一的例外情况是在合约内部使用原始的CALL操作码（除非你完全清楚自己在做什么，否则不建议这样做）。在这种情况下，对另一合约的CALL调用可能会失败，但如果父合约未对该错误进行检查和处理，那么执行流程将正常继续，交易结果也可能显示为成功。

Blocks 区块

All transactions are grouped together into “blocks.” A blockchain contains a series of such blocks that are chained together.所有交易都会被整合到“区块”中。区块链由一系列相互链接的此类区块组成。

In Ethereum, a block consists of:在以太坊中，一个区块包含以下内容：

  • the block header 区块头
  • information about the set of transactions included in that block关于该区块中包含的交易集合的信息
  • a set of other block headers for the current block’s ommers.当前区块叔伯区块的一组其他区块头

Ommers explained 叔块说明

What the heck is an “ommer?” An ommer is a block whose parent is equal to the current block’s parent’s parent. Let’s take a quick dive into what ommers are used for and why a block contains the block headers for ommers.“叔块”到底是什么？叔块是指父块与当前块的父块的父块相同的区块。我们来快速了解一下叔块的用途，以及为什么一个区块会包含叔块的区块头。

Because of the way Ethereum is built, block times are much lower (~15 seconds) than those of other blockchains, like Bitcoin (~10 minutes). This enables faster transaction processing. However, one of the downsides of shorter block times is that more competing block solutions are found by miners. These competing blocks are also referred to as “orphaned blocks” (i.e. mined blocks do not make it into the main chain).由于以太坊的构建方式，其出块时间（约15秒）远低于比特币等其他区块链（约10分钟），这使得交易处理速度更快。不过，更短的出块时间也存在一个弊端，即矿工挖掘出更多相互竞争的区块解决方案。这些相互竞争的区块也被称为“孤块”（即已挖掘的区块未能进入主链）。

The purpose of ommers is to help reward miners for including these orphaned blocks. The ommers that miners include must be “valid,” meaning within the sixth generation or smaller of the present block. After six children, stale orphaned blocks can no longer be referenced (because including older transactions would complicate things a bit).叔块的作用是奖励将这些孤块纳入区块的矿工。矿工纳入的叔块必须是“有效”的，即距离当前区块不超过第六代。经过六个子区块后，过时的孤块将无法再被引用（因为纳入更早的交易会使情况变得复杂）。

Ommer blocks receive a smaller reward than a full block. Nonetheless, there’s still some incentive for miners to include these orphaned blocks and reap a reward.奥默区块获得的奖励比完整区块少。尽管如此，矿工仍有一定动力将这些孤块纳入区块并获得奖励。

Block header 区块头

Let’s get back to blocks for a moment. We mentioned previously that every block has a block “header,” but what exactly is this? 我们再回到区块这个话题。我们之前提到过，每个区块都有一个区块“头部”，但它具体是什么呢？

A block header is a portion of the block consisting of:区块头是区块的一部分，由以下内容构成：

  • parentHash: a hash of the parent block’s header (this is what makes the block set a “chain”)父哈希：父区块头部的哈希值（这使得区块集成为一条“链”）
  • ommersHash: a hash of the current block’s list of ommers叔块哈希：当前区块叔块列表的哈希值
  • beneficiary: the account address that receives the fees for mining this block受益地址：接收该区块挖矿费用的账户地址
  • stateRoot: the hash of the root node of the state trie (recall how we learned that the state trie is stored in the header and makes it easy for light clients to verify anything about the state)状态根：状态树根节点的哈希（回顾我们所学的内容，状态树存储在区块头中，这使得轻客户端能够轻松验证与状态相关的任何信息）
  • transactionsRoot: the hash of the root node of the trie that contains all transactions listed in this block交易根：包含此区块中列出的所有交易的树状结构的根节点哈希值
  • receiptsRoot: the hash of the root node of the trie that contains the receipts of all transactions listed in this block收据根：包含此区块中所有交易收据的树状结构根节点的哈希值
  • logsBloom: a Bloom filter (data structure) that consists of log information日志布隆过滤器：一个由日志信息组成的 布隆过滤器（数据结构）
  • difficulty: the difficulty level of this block难度：该区块的难度等级
  • number: the count of current block (the genesis block has a block number of zero; the block number increases by 1 for each each subsequent block)编号：当前区块的计数（创世区块的区块编号为零；每个后续区块的区块编号依次加1）
  • gasLimit: the current gas limit per blockgasLimit：每个区块当前的燃气上限
  • gasUsed: the sum of the total gas used by transactions in this blockgasUsed：该区块中所有交易消耗的总燃气之和
  • timestamp: the unix timestamp of this block’s inception时间戳：该区块创建时的 Unix 时间戳
  • extraData: extra data related to this blockextraData：与该区块相关的额外数据
  • mixHash: a hash that, when combined with the nonce, proves that this block has carried out enough computation混合哈希：一种与随机数结合后，可证明该区块已完成足够计算量的哈希值
  • nonce: a hash that, when combined with the mixHash, proves that this block has carried out enough computation随机数：一种与混合哈希值结合后，可证明该区块已完成足够计算量的哈希值

Notice how every block header contains three trie structures for:请注意每个区块头都包含以下三种用于以下用途的树结构：

  • state (stateRoot) 状态（状态根）
  • transactions (transactionsRoot) 交易（交易根）
  • receipts (receiptsRoot) 收据（收据根）

These trie structures are nothing but the Merkle Patricia tries we discussed earlier.这些特里树结构正是我们之前讨论过的默克尔帕特里夏树。

Additionally, there are a few terms from the above description that are worth clarifying. Let’s take a look.此外，上述描述中有几个术语值得进一步说明，我们来逐一探讨。

Logs 日志

Ethereum allows for logs to make it possible to track various transactions and messages. A contract can explicitly generate a log by defining “events” that it wants to log.以太坊支持日志功能，这使得追踪各类交易和消息成为可能。智能合约可通过定义需要记录的“事件”来显式生成日志。

A log entry contains: 一条日志条目包含：

  • the logger’s account address, 记录器的账户地址
  • a series of topics that represent various events carried out by this transaction, and一系列代表该交易执行的各类事件的主题，以及
  • any data associated with these events.与这些事件相关的任何数据。

Logs are stored in a bloom filter, which stores the endless log data in an efficient manner.日志存储在布隆过滤器中，该过滤器能高效存储海量日志数据。

Transaction receipt 交易收据

Logs stored in the header come from the log information contained in the transaction receipt. Just as you receive a receipt when you buy something at a store, Ethereum generates a receipt for every transaction. Like you’d expect, each receipt contains certain information about the transaction. This receipt includes items like:存储在日志头中的信息来源于交易收据里的日志数据。就像你在商店购物时会拿到一张收据一样，以太坊会为每一笔交易生成一份收据。正如你所预料的那样，每份收据都包含着该笔交易的相关信息。该收据包含以下内容：

  • the block number 区块编号
  • block hash 区块哈希
  • transaction hash 交易哈希
  • gas used by the current transaction当前交易消耗的燃气
  • cumulative gas used in the current block after the current transaction has executed当前交易执行后在当前区块中消耗的累计燃气
  • logs created when executing the current transaction执行当前交易时创建的日志
  • ..and so on ……以此类推

Block difficulty 区块难度

The “difficulty” of a block is used to enforce consistency in the time it takes to validate blocks. The genesis block has a difficulty of 131,072, and a special formula is used to calculate the difficulty of every block thereafter. If a certain block is validated more quickly than the previous block, the Ethereum protocol increases that block’s difficulty.区块的“难度”用于确保区块验证时间的一致性。创世区块的难度为131072，此后每个区块的难度都通过一个特定公式计算得出。如果某个区块的验证速度快于前一个区块，以太坊协议会提高该区块的难度。

The difficulty of the block affects the nonce, which is a hash that must be calculated when mining a block, using the proof-of-work algorithm.区块的难度会影响随机数，随机数是在通过工作量证明算法挖掘区块时必须计算的哈希值。

The relationship between the block’s difficulty and nonce is mathematically formalized as:区块的难度与随机数之间的关系在数学上被形式化为：

where Hd is the difficulty. 其中 Hd 为难度。

The only way to find a nonce that meets a difficulty threshold is to use the proof-of-work algorithm to enumerate all of the possibilities. The expected time to find a solution is proportional to the difficulty — the higher the difficulty, the harder it becomes to find the nonce, and so the harder it is to validate the block, which in turn increases the time it takes to validate a new block. So, by adjusting the difficulty of a block, the protocol can adjust how long it takes to validate a block.找到满足难度阈值的随机数（nonce）的唯一方法是使用工作量证明算法枚举所有可能性。找到解的预期时间与难度成正比——难度越高，找到随机数就越困难，验证区块也就越困难，进而延长验证新区块所需的时间。因此，通过调整区块的难度，协议可以控制验证区块所需的时间。

If, on the other hand, validation time is getting slower, the protocol decreases the difficulty. In this way, the validation time self-adjusts to maintain a constant rate — on average, one block every 15 seconds.另一方面，如果验证时间变慢，协议会降低难度。通过这种方式，验证时间会自动调整以保持恒定速率——平均每15秒出一个区块。

Transaction Execution 交易执行

We’ve come to one of the most complex parts of the Ethereum protocol: the execution of a transaction. Say you send a transaction off into the Ethereum network to be processed. What happens to transition the state of Ethereum to include your transaction?我们来到了以太坊协议中最复杂的部分之一：交易的执行。假设你向以太坊网络发送一笔交易等待处理。以太坊的状态要如何更新，才能将你的交易纳入其中呢？

First, all transactions must meet an initial set of requirements in order to be executed. These include:首先，所有交易必须满足一组初始要求才能执行。具体包括：

  • The transaction must be a properly formatted RLP. “RLP” stands for “Recursive Length Prefix” and is a data format used to encode nested arrays of binary data. RLP is the format Ethereum uses to serialize objects.该交易必须是格式正确的 RLP。“RLP” 代表“递归长度前缀”，是一种用于对二进制数据的嵌套数组进行编码的数据格式。RLP 是以太坊用于序列化对象的格式。
  • Valid transaction signature. 有效的交易签名。
  • Valid transaction nonce. Recall that the nonce of an account is the count of transactions sent from that account. To be valid, a transaction nonce must be equal to the sender account’s nonce.有效的交易随机数。请记住，账户的随机数是指从该账户发送的交易数量。要使交易随机数有效，其值必须等于发送方账户的随机数。
  • The transaction’s gas limit must be equal to or greater than the intrinsic gas used by the transaction. The intrinsic gas includes:交易的 gas 上限必须大于或等于交易使用的 固有 gas。固有 gas 包括：
  • 1) a predefined cost of 21,000 gas for executing the transaction1) 执行交易的预定义燃气费为21000燃气
  • 2) a gas fee for data sent with the transaction (4 gas for every byte of data or code that equals zero, and 68 gas for every non-zero byte of data or code)2) 交易附带数据的燃气费（数据或代码中每个零值字节消耗4燃气，每个非零值字节消耗68燃气）
  • 3) if the transaction is a contract-creating transaction, an additional 32,000 gas3) 若该交易为创建合约的交易，需额外支付 32,000 燃气
  • 4) gas cost of each operation performed by transaction (see Appendix G) 4) 交易执行的每个操作的燃气成本（见附录G）
  • The sender’s account balance must have enough Ether to cover the “upfront” gas costs that the sender must pay. The calculation for the upfront gas cost is simple: First, the transaction’s gas limit is multiplied by the transaction’s gas price to determine the maximum gas cost. Then, this maximum cost is added to the total value being transferred from the sender to the recipient.发送方的账户余额必须有足够的以太币来支付发送方必须缴纳的“预付”燃气费。预付燃气费的计算方法很简单：首先，将交易的燃气上限乘以交易的燃气价格，得出最大燃气费。然后，将该最大费用加上从发送方转移到接收方的总金额。

If the transaction meets all of the above requirements for validity, then we move onto the next step.如果该交易满足上述所有有效性要求，我们将进入下一步。

First, we deduct the upfront cost of execution from the sender’s balance, and increase the nonce of the sender’s account by 1 to account for the current transaction. At this point, we can calculate the gas remaining as the total gas limit for the transaction minus the intrinsic gas used.首先，我们从发送方的余额中扣除执行的前期成本，并将发送方账户的随机数加1，以计入当前交易。此时，我们可以将剩余燃气计算为交易的总燃气上限减去已使用的固有燃气。

Next, the transaction starts executing. Throughout the execution of a transaction, Ethereum keeps track of the “substate.” This substate is a way to record information accrued during the transaction that will be needed immediately after the transaction completes. Specifically, it contains:接下来，交易开始执行。在交易的整个执行过程中，以太坊会持续追踪“子状态”。子状态是一种用于记录交易执行过程中产生、且在交易完成后需立即使用的信息的机制。具体来说，它包含以下内容：

  • Self-destruct set: a set of accounts (if any) that will be discarded after the transaction completes.自毁账户集：指交易完成后将被弃用的一组账户（如有）。
  • Log series: archived and indexable checkpoints of the virtual machine’s code execution.日志系列：虚拟机代码执行的可归档且可索引的检查点。
  • Refund balance: the amount to be refunded to the sender account after the transaction. Remember how we mentioned that storage in Ethereum costs money, and that a sender is refunded for clearing up storage? Ethereum keeps track of this using a refund counter. The refund counter starts at zero and increments every time the contract deletes something in storage.余额退款：交易完成后将退还给发送方账户的金额。还记得我们提到过以太坊上的存储需要付费，而发送方在清理存储时会获得退款吗？以太坊通过退款计数器来追踪这一金额。该计数器初始值为零，每当合约删除存储中的内容时，计数器就会增加。

Next, the various computations required by the transaction are processed.接下来，交易所需的各项计算将被处理。

Once all the steps required by the transaction have been processed, and assuming there is no invalid state, the state is finalized by determining the amount of unused gas to be refunded to the sender. In addition to the unused gas, the sender is also refunded some allowance from the “refund balance” that we described above.当交易所需的所有步骤均已处理完毕，且假设不存在无效状态时，系统会通过确定需退还给发送方的未使用燃气量来完成状态的最终确认。除未使用的燃气外，发送方还会从我们上文所述的“退款余额”中获得部分额度的退款。

Once the sender is refunded: 当发送方收到退款后：

  • the Ether for the gas is given to the miner用于支付燃气的以太币会支付给矿工
  • the gas used by the transaction is added to the block gas counter (which keeps track of the total gas used by all transactions in the block, and is useful when validating a block)交易消耗的 gas 会被添加到区块 gas 计数器中（该计数器会记录区块中所有交易消耗的总 gas，在验证区块时十分有用）
  • all accounts in the self-destruct set (if any) are deleted自 destruct 集合中的所有账户（如有）均被删除

Finally, we’re left with the new state and a set of the logs created by the transaction.最终，我们得到新的状态以及由该交易创建的一组日志。

Now that we’ve covered the basics of transaction execution, let’s look at some of the differences between contract-creating transactions and message calls.既然我们已经讲完了交易执行的基础知识，接下来我们来看看创建合约的交易与消息调用之间的一些区别。

Contract creation 合约创建

Recall that in Ethereum, there are two types of accounts: contract accounts and externally owned accounts. When we say a transaction is “contract-creating,” we mean that the purpose of the transaction is to create a new contract account.回想一下，在以太坊中存在两种账户类型：合约账户和外部拥有账户。当我们说一笔交易是“创建合约交易”时，指的是该交易的目的是创建一个新的合约账户。

In order to create a new contract account, we first declare the address of the new account using a special formula. Then we initialize the new account by:要创建新的合约账户，我们首先使用特殊公式声明新账户的地址。然后通过以下方式初始化新账户：

  • Setting the nonce to zero 将随机数设为零
  • If the sender sent some amount of Ether as value with the transaction, setting the account balance to that value如果发送方在交易中以价值的形式发送了一定数量的以太币，则将账户余额设置为该数值
  • Deducting the value added to this new account’s balance from the sender’s balance从发送方的余额中扣除添加到这个新账户余额中的金额
  • Setting the storage as empty 将存储设置为空
  • Setting the contract’s codeHash as the hash of an empty string将合约的 codeHash 设置为空字符串的哈希值

Once we initialize the account, we can actually create the account, using the init code sent with the transaction (see the “Transaction and messages” section for a refresher on the init code). What happens during the execution of this init code is varied. Depending on the constructor of the contract, it might update the account’s storage, create other contract accounts, make other message calls, etc.初始化账户后，我们实际上可以通过交易附带的初始化代码来创建该账户（有关初始化代码的复习内容，请参见“交易与消息”部分）。这段初始化代码在执行过程中会发生不同的操作。根据合约的构造函数，它可能会更新账户的存储、创建其他合约账户、发起其他消息调用等。

As the code to initialize a contract is executed, it uses gas. The transaction is not allowed to use up more gas than the remaining gas. If it does, the execution will hit an out-of-gas (OOG) exception and exit. If the transaction exits due to an out-of-gas exception, then the state is reverted to the point immediately prior to transaction. The sender is not refunded the gas that was spent before running out.执行初始化合约的代码时会消耗燃气。交易消耗的燃气不允许超过剩余燃气，否则执行将触发燃气耗尽（OOG）异常并终止。若交易因燃气耗尽异常终止，状态会回滚至交易执行前的瞬间，且发送方不会获得燃气耗尽前已消耗燃气的退款。

Boo hoo. 真倒霉。

However, if the sender sent any Ether value with the transaction, the Ether value will be refunded even if the contract creation fails. Phew!不过，如果发送方在交易中附带了任何以太币金额，即便合约创建失败，这笔以太币金额也会被退回。呼！

If the initialization code executes successfully, a final contract-creation cost is paid. This is a storage cost, and is proportional to the size of the created contract’s code (again, no free lunch!) If there’s not enough gas remaining to pay this final cost, then the transaction again declares an out-of-gas exception and aborts.如果初始化代码执行成功，将支付最终的合约创建成本。这是一种存储成本，与所创建合约的代码大小成正比（再次强调，天下没有免费的午餐！）。若剩余的 gas 不足以支付该最终成本，交易将再次触发 gas 耗尽异常并终止。

If all goes well and we make it this far without exceptions, then any remaining unused gas is refunded to the original sender of the transaction, and the altered state is now allowed to persist!如果一切顺利，我们一路执行下来没有出现异常，那么所有剩余未使用的 gas 都会退还给交易的原始发送方，且修改后的状态现在可以永久保留！

Hooray! 太棒了！

Message calls 消息调用

The execution of a message call is similar to that of a contract creation, with a few differences.消息调用的执行过程与合约创建的执行过程类似，但存在一些差异。

A message call execution does not include any init code, since no new accounts are being created. However, it can contain input data, if this data was provided by the transaction sender. Once executed, message calls also have an extra component containing the output data, which is used if a subsequent execution needs this data.消息调用执行不包含任何初始化代码，因为不会创建任何新账户。不过，如果交易发送方提供了输入数据，消息调用可包含该数据。执行完成后，消息调用还会额外包含一个输出数据组件，若后续执行需要该数据，便会使用它。

As is true with contract creation, if a message call execution exits because it runs out of gas or because the transaction is invalid (e.g. stack overflow, invalid jump destination, or invalid instruction), none of the gas used is refunded to the original caller. Instead, all of the remaining unused gas is consumed, and the state is reset to the point immediately prior to balance transfer.与合约创建的情况一样，如果消息调用执行因燃气耗尽或交易无效（例如栈溢出、无效跳转目标或无效指令）而退出，所消耗的燃气不会退还给原始调用方。相反，所有剩余未使用的燃气都会被消耗，且状态会重置到转账之前的瞬间。

Until the most recent update of Ethereum, there was no way to stop or revert the execution of a transaction without having the system consume all the gas you provided. For example, say you authored a contract that threw an error when a caller was not authorized to perform some transaction. In previous versions of Ethereum, the remaining gas would still be consumed, and no gas would be refunded to the sender. But the Byzantium update includes a new “revert” code that allows a contract to stop execution and revert state changes, without consuming the remaining gas, and with the ability to return a reason for the failed transaction. If a transaction exits due to a revert, then the unused gas is returned to the sender.在以太坊最近一次更新之前，无法在不让系统消耗你提供的所有 gas 的情况下停止或回滚交易的执行。例如，假设你编写了一个合约，当调用者未被授权执行某笔交易时，该合约会抛出错误。在以太坊的早期版本中，剩余的 gas 仍会被消耗，且不会向发送者退还任何 gas。但拜占庭版本更新引入了一种新的“回滚”代码，允许合约停止执行并回滚状态更改，同时不消耗剩余 gas，还能返回交易失败的原因。如果一笔交易因回滚而终止，未使用的 gas 将退还给发送者。

Execution model 执行模型

So far, we’ve learned about the series of steps that have to happen for a transaction to execute from start to finish. Now, we’ll look at how the transaction actually executes within the VM.到目前为止，我们已经了解了一笔交易从启动到完成所需经历的一系列步骤。接下来，我们将探讨交易在虚拟机（VM）中的实际执行过程。

The part of the protocol that actually handles processing the transactions is Ethereum’s own virtual machine, known as the Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM).协议中实际处理交易的部分是以太坊独有的虚拟机，即以太坊虚拟机（EVM）。

The EVM is a Turing complete virtual machine, as defined earlier. The only limitation the EVM has that a typical Turing complete machine does not is that the EVM is intrinsically bound by gas. Thus, the total amount of computation that can be done is intrinsically limited by the amount of gas provided.如前文所定义，EVM 是一台图灵完备的虚拟机。EVM 相较于典型的图灵完备机器，唯一的局限性在于其本质上与燃气（gas）绑定。因此，可执行的计算总量本质上受所提供燃气数量的限制。

Moreover, the EVM has a stack-based architecture. A stack machine is a computer that uses a last-in, first-out stack to hold temporary values.此外，EVM 采用基于栈的架构。栈机是一种使用后进先出栈来存储临时值的计算机。

The size of each stack item in the EVM is 256-bit, and the stack has a maximum size of 1024.EVM 中每个栈项的大小为 256 位，栈的最大大小为 1024。

The EVM has memory, where items are stored as word-addressed byte arrays. Memory is volatile, meaning it is not permanent.EVM 拥有内存，其中的项目以字寻址字节数组的形式存储。内存是易失性的，这意味着它不是永久性的。

The EVM also has storage. Unlike memory, storage is non-volatile and is maintained as part of the system state. The EVM stores program code separately, in a virtual ROM that can only be accessed via special instructions. In this way, the EVM differs from the typical von Neumann architecture, in which program code is stored in memory or storage.EVM 还具备存储功能。与内存不同，存储是非易失性的，且作为系统状态的一部分被保留。EVM 将程序代码单独存储在一个虚拟ROM中，该虚拟ROM只能通过特殊指令进行访问。通过这种方式，EVM 有别于典型的冯·诺依曼架构——在冯·诺依曼架构中，程序代码存储在内存或存储设备中。

The EVM also has its own language: “EVM bytecode.” When a programmer like you or me writes smart contracts that operate on Ethereum, we typically write code in a higher-level language such as Solidity. We can then compile that down to EVM bytecode that the EVM can understand.EVM 还有自己的语言：“EVM 字节码”。当像你我这样的程序员编写在以太坊上运行的智能合约时，我们通常会用 Solidity 这类高级语言编写代码，之后再将其编译成 EVM 能够理解的 EVM 字节码。

Okay, now on to execution. 好的，现在来讲解执行过程。

Before executing a particular computation, the processor makes sure that the following information is available and valid:在执行特定计算之前，处理器会确保以下信息可用且有效：

  • System state 系统状态
  • Remaining gas for computation 计算剩余的Gas
  • Address of the account that owns the code that is executing执行代码的账户地址
  • Address of the sender of the transaction that originated this execution发起本次执行的交易的发送方地址
  • Address of the account that caused the code to execute (could be different from the original sender)触发代码执行的账户地址（可能与原始发送方不同）
  • Gas price of the transaction that originated this execution发起本次执行的交易的燃气价格
  • Input data for this execution 本次执行的输入数据
  • Value (in Wei) passed to this account as part of the current execution本次执行中传递给该账户的价值（以 Wei 为单位）
  • Machine code to be executed 待执行的机器码
  • Block header of the current block 当前区块的区块头
  • Depth of the present message call or contract creation stack当前消息调用或合约创建栈的深度

At the start of execution, memory and stack are empty and the program counter is zero.执行开始时，内存和栈均为空，程序计数器为零。

PC: 0 STACK: [] MEM: [], STORAGE: {}程序计数器：0 栈：[] 内存：[] 存储：{}

The EVM then executes the transaction recursively, computing the system state and the machine state for each loop. The system state is simply Ethereum’s global state. The machine state is comprised of:EVM 随后递归执行该交易，在每次循环中计算系统状态和机器状态。系统状态即以太坊的全局状态。机器状态由以下部分组成：

  • gas available 可用gas
  • program counter 程序计数器
  • memory contents 内存内容
  • active number of words in memory 内存中的有效字数量
  • stack contents. 栈内容。

Stack items are added or removed from the leftmost portion of the series.栈项从序列的最左侧部分进行添加或移除。

On each cycle, the appropriate gas amount is reduced from the remaining gas, and the program counter increments.在每个周期，从剩余气体量中减去相应的气体量，同时程序计数器递增。

At the end of each loop, there are three possibilities:在每次循环结束时，存在三种可能性：

  1. The machine reaches an exceptional state (e.g. insufficient gas, invalid instructions, insufficient stack items, stack items would overflow above 1024, invalid JUMP/JUMPI destination, etc.) and so must be halted, with any changes discarded机器进入异常状态（例如燃气不足、指令无效、栈项不足、栈项将溢出超过1024、无效的JUMP/JUMPI目标地址等），因此必须停止运行，且所有更改均会被丢弃
  2. The sequence continues to process into the next loop该序列继续处理进入下一个循环
  3. The machine reaches a controlled halt (the end of the execution process)机器进入受控停止状态（执行过程的结束）

Assuming the execution doesn’t hit an exceptional state and reaches a “controlled” or normal halt, the machine generates the resultant state, the remaining gas after this execution, the accrued substate, and the resultant output.假设执行未触发异常状态并达到“受控”或正常终止状态，机器会生成结果状态、本次执行后的剩余Gas、已产生的子状态以及结果输出。

Phew. We got through one of the most complex parts of Ethereum. Even if you didn’t fully comprehend this part, that’s okay. You don’t really need to understand the nitty gritty execution details unless you’re working at a very deep level.呼。我们已经完成了以太坊中最复杂的部分之一。即便你没有完全理解这部分也没关系。除非你从事非常深入的研究工作，否则你并不真正需要去了解那些错综复杂的执行细节。

How a block gets finalized 区块如何完成最终确认

Finally, let’s look at how a block of many transactions gets finalized.最后，我们来看看包含多笔交易的区块是如何被最终确认的。

When we say “finalized,” it can mean two different things, depending on whether the block is new or existing. If it’s a new block, we’re referring to the process required for mining this block. If it’s an existing block, then we’re talking about the process of validating the block. In either case, there are four requirements for a block to be “finalized”:当我们说“已完成”时，根据区块是新的还是已存在的，它可能有两种不同的含义。如果是新区块，我们指的是挖掘该区块所需的流程。如果是现有区块，那么我们讨论的是验证该区块的流程。无论哪种情况，一个区块要“已完成”都需要满足四个条件：

Each ommer block within the block header must be a valid header and be within the sixth generation of the present block.区块头中的每个 ommer 区块必须是有效的区块头，且位于当前区块的第六代之内。

The gasUsed number on the block must be equal to the cumulative gas used by the transactions listed in the block. (Recall that when executing a transaction, we keep track of the block gas counter, which keeps track of the total gas used by all transactions in the block).区块上的gasUsed数值必须等于该区块中列出的交易所消耗的累计燃气。（回想一下，在执行交易时，我们会跟踪区块燃气计数器，该计数器会记录区块中所有交易消耗的总燃气量）。

The beneficiary address is awarded 5 Ether for mining the block. (Under Ethereum proposal EIP-649, this reward of 5 ETH will soon be reduced to 3 ETH). Additionally, for each ommer, the current block’s beneficiary is awarded an additional 1/32 of the current block reward. Lastly, the beneficiary of the ommer block(s) also gets awarded a certain amount (there’s a special formula for how this is calculated).出块受益地址将获得5个以太币的区块挖矿奖励。（根据以太坊改进提案EIP-649，这5个以太币的奖励很快将降至3个以太币）。此外，每存在一个叔块，当前区块的受益地址还会额外获得当前区块奖励的1/32。最后，叔块的受益地址也会获得一定数额的奖励（其计算方式有专门的公式）。

Ensure that all transactions and resultant state changes are applied, and then define the new block as the state after the block reward has been applied to the final transaction’s resultant state. Verification occurs by checking this final state against the state trie stored in the header.确保所有交易及由此产生的状态变更均已执行，随后将新区块定义为区块奖励应用于最终交易结果状态后的新状态。验证过程通过将该最终状态与区块头中存储的状态树进行比对来完成。

Mining proof of work 工作量证明挖矿

The “Blocks” section briefly addressed the concept of block difficulty. The algorithm that gives meaning to block difficulty is called Proof of Work (PoW).“区块”部分简要阐述了区块难度的概念。赋予区块难度实际意义的算法被称为工作量证明（Proof of Work，简称 PoW）。

Ethereum’s proof-of-work algorithm is called “Ethash” (previously known as Dagger-Hashimoto).以太坊的工作量证明算法被称为“Ethash”（此前名为 Dagger-Hashimoto）。

The algorithm is formally defined as:该算法的正式定义为：

where m is the mixHash, n is the nonce, Hn is the new block’s header (excluding the nonce and mixHash components, which have to be computed), Hn is the nonce of the block header, and d is the DAG, which is a large data set.其中 m 为 混合哈希值， n 为 随机数， Hₙ 为新区块的区块头（不含需计算的 随机数 和 混合哈希值 组件）， Hₙ 为区块头的随机数， d 为 DAG（一种大型数据集）。

In the “Blocks” section, we talked about the various items that exist in a block header. Two of those components were called the mixHash and the nonce. As you may recall:在“区块”部分，我们讨论了区块头中存在的各种项。其中两个组件被称为混合哈希和随机数。你可能还记得：

  • mixHash is a hash that, when combined with the nonce, proves that this block has carried out enough computation混合哈希是一种哈希值，它与随机数结合后，可证明该区块已完成了足够的计算量。
  • nonce is a hash that, when combined with the mixHash, proves that this block has carried out enough computation随机数是一种哈希值，它与混合哈希值（mixHash）结合后，可证明该区块已完成了足够的计算量。

The PoW function is used to evaluate these two items.PoW 函数用于验证这两个参数。

How exactly the mixHash and nonce are calculated using the PoW function is somewhat complex, and something we can delve deeper into in a separate post. But at a high level, it works like this:通过 PoW 函数计算 mixHash 和 nonce 的具体过程有些复杂，我们可以在另一篇文章中深入探讨。但从整体来看，其工作原理如下：

A “seed” is calculated for each block. This seed is different for every “epoch,” where each epoch is 30,000 blocks long. For the first epoch, the seed is the hash of a series of 32 bytes of zeros. For every subsequent epoch, it is the hash of the previous seed hash. Using this seed, a node can calculate a pseudo-random “cache.”每个区块都会计算一个“种子”。该种子在每个“纪元”中都不同，每个纪元包含30,000个区块。第一个纪元的种子为32字节全零序列的哈希值，而后续每个纪元的种子则为前一个种子哈希值的哈希值。节点可利用该种子计算出一个伪随机“缓存”。

This cache is incredibly useful because it enables the concept of “light nodes,” which we discussed previously in this post. The purpose of light nodes is to afford certain nodes the ability to efficiently verify a transaction without the burden of storing the entire blockchain dataset. A light node can verify the validity of a transaction based solely on this cache, because the cache can regenerate the specific block it needs to verify.这个缓存的用途极大，因为它实现了我们在本文前文讨论过的“轻节点”这一概念。轻节点的作用是让部分节点能够高效验证交易，同时无需承担存储整个区块链数据集的负担。轻节点仅依靠该缓存即可验证交易的有效性，因为缓存能够重新生成其需要验证的特定区块。

Using the cache, a node can generate the DAG “dataset,” where each item in the dataset depends on a small number of pseudo-randomly-selected items from the cache. In order to be a miner, you must generate this full dataset; all full clients and miners store this dataset, and the dataset grows linearly with time.利用缓存，节点可以生成有向无环图（DAG）“数据集”，数据集中的每个项都依赖于缓存中少量伪随机选择的项。要成为矿工，必须生成完整的该数据集；所有全节点客户端和矿工都会存储该数据集，且数据集会随时间线性增长。

Miners can then take random slices of the dataset and put them through a mathematical function to hash them together into a “mixHash.” A miner will repeatedly generate a mixHash until the output is below the desired target nonce. When the output meets this requirement, this nonce is considered valid and the block can be added to the chain.随后，矿工可以选取数据集的随机片段，将其通过一个数学函数进行哈希运算，将它们合并成一个“混合哈希值”。矿工会反复生成混合哈希值，直到输出结果低于目标随机数。当输出满足这一条件时，该随机数即被视为有效，对应的区块便可添加到区块链上。

Mining as a security mechanism 作为安全机制的挖矿

‍Overall, the purpose of the PoW is to prove, in a cryptographically secure way, that a particular amount of computation has been expended to generate some output (i.e. the nonce). This is because there is no better way to find a nonce that is below the required threshold other than to enumerate all the possibilities. The outputs of repeatedly applying the hash function have a uniform distribution, and so we can be assured that, on average, the time needed to find such a nonce depends on the difficulty threshold. The higher the difficulty, the longer it takes to solve for the nonce. In this way, the PoW algorithm gives meaning to the concept of difficulty, which is used to enforce blockchain security.‍总体而言，工作量证明（PoW）的目的是以密码学安全的方式证明，为生成某一输出（即随机数）已消耗了特定数量的计算资源。这是因为除了枚举所有可能性之外，没有更好的方法能找到低于要求阈值的随机数。重复应用哈希函数的输出呈均匀分布，因此我们可以确定，平均而言，找到此类随机数所需的时间取决于难度阈值。难度越高，求解随机数所需的时间就越长。通过这种方式，工作量证明算法赋予了“难度”这一概念实际意义，而该概念被用于保障区块链的安全性。

What do we mean by blockchain security? It’s simple: we want to create a blockchain that EVERYONE trusts. As we discussed previously in this post, if more than one chain existed, users would lose trust, because they would be unable to reasonably determine which chain was the “valid” chain. In order for a group of users to accept the underlying state that is stored on a blockchain, we need a single canonical blockchain that a group of people believes in.我们所说的区块链安全是什么意思？很简单：我们要打造一条让所有人都信任的区块链。正如我们在本文之前讨论过的，如果存在多条链，用户就会失去信任，因为他们无法合理判断哪条链是“有效”的链。要让一组用户认可存储在区块链上的基础状态，我们就需要一条能让这群人共同认可的、唯一的标准区块链。

This is exactly what the PoW algorithm does: it ensures that a particular blockchain will remain canonical into the future, making it incredibly difficult for an attacker to create new blocks that overwrite a certain part of history (e.g. by erasing transactions or creating fake transactions) or maintain a fork. To have their block validated first, an attacker would need to consistently solve for the nonce faster than anyone else in the network, such that the network believes their chain is the heaviest chain (based on the principles of the GHOST protocol we mentioned earlier). This would be impossible unless the attacker had more than half of the network mining power, a scenario known as the majority 51% attack.这正是工作量证明（PoW）算法的作用：它确保某条区块链在未来保持主链地位，让攻击者极难创建新区块来篡改部分历史（比如抹去交易或伪造交易），也难以维持一条分叉链。 攻击者若想让自己的区块率先被验证，就必须持续比网络中其他所有节点更快地求解随机数（nonce），从而让网络认为其链是最长链（基于我们之前提到的GHOST协议原则）。除非攻击者掌握了网络超过一半的算力，否则这是不可能实现的，这种情况被称为51%多数攻击。

Mining as a wealth distribution mechanism作为财富分配机制的挖矿

Beyond providing a secure blockchain, PoW is also a way to distribute wealth to those who expend their computation for providing this security. Recall that a miner receives a reward for mining a block, including:除了提供安全的区块链之外，工作量证明（PoW）也是一种将财富分配给那些投入算力来提供这种安全的人的方式。回顾一下，矿工因挖掘区块而获得奖励，包括：

  • a static block reward of 5 ether for the “winning’” block (soon to be changed to 3 ether)给“获胜”区块的静态区块奖励</b>为5以太币（很快将调整为3以太币）
  • the cost of gas expended within the block by the transactions included in the block区块内包含的交易在区块内消耗的燃气费用
  • an extra reward for including ommers as part of the block将叔块纳入区块所获得的额外奖励

In order to ensure that the use of the PoW consensus mechanism for security and wealth distribution is sustainable in the long run, Ethereum strives to instill these two properties:为确保 PoW 共识机制在安全与财富分配方面的长期可持续使用，以太坊致力于注入以下两种特性：

  • Make it accessible to as many people as possible. In other words, people shouldn’t need specialized or uncommon hardware to run the algorithm. The purpose of this is to make the wealth distribution model as open as possible so that anyone can provide any amount of compute power in return for Ether.让尽可能多的人都能使用它。也就是说，人们不需要专门或罕见的硬件就能运行该算法。这样做的目的是让财富分配模型尽可能开放，以便任何人都可以提供任意数量的算力来换取以太币。
  • Reduce the possibility for any single node (or small set) to make a disproportionate amount of profit. Any node that can make a disproportionate amount of profit means that the node has a large influence on determining the canonical blockchain. This is troublesome because it reduces network security.降低任意单个节点（或少数节点集合）获取不成比例利润的可能性。任何能获取不成比例利润的节点，都意味着其在确定区块链规范版本方面具有重大影响力。这会带来问题，因为它会削弱网络的安全性。

In the Bitcoin blockchain network, one problem that arises in relation to the above two properties is that the PoW algorithm is a SHA256 hash function. The weakness with this type of function is that it can be solved much more efficiently using specialized hardware, also known as ASICs.在比特币区块链网络中，与上述两个特性相关的一个问题是，工作量证明（PoW）算法采用的是SHA256哈希函数。这类函数的缺陷在于，借助被称为专用集成电路（ASICs）的专用硬件可以更高效地破解它。

In order to mitigate this issue, Ethereum has chosen to make its PoW algorithm (Ethhash) sequentially memory-hard. This means that the algorithm is engineered so that calculating the nonce requires a lot of memory AND bandwidth. The large memory requirements make it hard for a computer to use its memory in parallel to discover multiple nonces simultaneously, and the high bandwidth requirements make it difficult for even a super-fast computer to discover multiple nonce simultaneously. This reduces the risk of centralization and creates a more level playing field for the nodes that are doing the verification.为了缓解这一问题，以太坊选择让其工作量证明算法（Ethhash）具备顺序内存硬特性。这意味着该算法的设计使得计算随机数需要大量内存和带宽。巨大的内存需求让计算机难以并行利用内存来同时寻找多个随机数，而高带宽需求即便对于超高速计算机来说，也难以同时找到多个随机数。这降低了中心化的风险，为执行验证的节点创造了更公平的竞争环境。

One thing to note is that Ethereum is transitioning from a PoW consensus mechanism to something called “proof-of-stake”. This is a beastly topic of its own that we can hopefully explore in a future post. ☺️需要注意的一点是，以太坊正从工作量证明（PoW）共识机制向“权益证明”过渡。这本身就是一个相当复杂的话题，希望我们能在未来的文章中对此展开探讨。☺️

Conclusion 结语

…Phew! You made it to the end. I hope?呼！你终于看到结尾了。但愿如此？

There’s a lot to digest in this post, I know. If it takes you multiple reads to fully understand what’s going on, that’s totally fine. I personally read the Ethereum yellow paper, white paper, and various parts of the code base many times before grokking what was going on.我知道这篇帖子里有很多内容需要消化。如果你需要读好几遍才能完全理解其中的内容，完全没关系。我个人在搞懂以太坊黄皮书、白皮书以及代码库的各个部分之前，也反复阅读了很多次。

Nonetheless, I hope you found this overview helpful. If you find any errors or mistakes, I’d love for you to write a private note or post it directly in the comments. I look at all of ’em, I promise ;)不过，我希望这份概述能对你有所帮助。如果你发现任何错误，欢迎你发私信告诉我，或者直接发布在评论区。我会看所有留言的，我保证；）

And remember, I’m human (yep, it’s true) and I make mistakes. I took the time to write this post for the benefit of the community, for free. So please be constructive in your feedback, without unnecessary bashing. 记住，我也是普通人（没错，这是真的），也会犯错。我花时间写下这篇帖子，免费为社区提供帮助。所以请在反馈时多提建设性意见，不要进行无意义的抨击。
